questions.q: Sabarmati Ashram is located on the bank of which river?
questions.explanation: The ashram takes its name from the Sabarmati river, on whose bank it was established.

questions.q: Sabarmati Ashram is situated near which Gujarati city?
questions.explanation: Sabarmati Ashram lies on the outskirts of Ahmedabad in Gujarat.

questions.q: Who established the Sabarmati Ashram?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi established the ashram on the bank of the Sabarmati river.

questions.q: Which famous march set out from Sabarmati Ashram in 1930?
questions.explanation: The historic Dandi Salt March began at Sabarmati Ashram in 1930.

questions.q: In which Indian state is Sabarmati Ashram located?
questions.explanation: Sabarmati Ashram is in Gujarat, near the city of Ahmedabad.

questions.q: Sabarmati Ashram served as a centre for which kinds of activity under Gandhi?
questions.explanation: The ashram became a centre of Gandhi's constructive social work and his political campaigns.

questions.q: Sabarmati Ashram is closely associated with which subtitle reflecting its purpose?
questions.explanation: The ashram is known as 'The Abode of Service', reflecting its dedication to service and constructive work.

questions.q: Which destination did the Dandi March that left Sabarmati Ashram aim to reach?
questions.explanation: The marchers set out from Sabarmati Ashram for the coastal village of Dandi, where Gandhi broke the salt law.

questions.q: Activities at Sabarmati Ashram included the promotion of which hand-based craft?
questions.explanation: Spinning and weaving of khadi were central to the self-reliant life Gandhi promoted at the ashram.

questions.q: Sabarmati Ashram is preserved today mainly as what?
questions.explanation: The site is preserved as a memorial and museum dedicated to Gandhi and his work.

questions.q: In which year did Gandhi establish the ashram on the bank of the Sabarmati river?
questions.explanation: Gandhi established the Sabarmati Ashram on the river bank in 1917.

questions.q: Before moving to the Sabarmati site, Gandhi had first set up his ashram in 1915 at which location near Ahmedabad?
questions.explanation: Gandhi first founded an ashram at Kochrab in 1915 before relocating it to the Sabarmati river bank in 1917.

questions.q: On which date did the Dandi March set out from Sabarmati Ashram?
questions.explanation: Gandhi and his followers began the Dandi March from the ashram on 12 March 1930.

questions.q: Sabarmati Ashram is also commonly known by which other name?
questions.explanation: The ashram is also called the Gandhi Ashram and was known as the Harijan Ashram.

questions.q: Gandhi's own cottage at Sabarmati Ashram is preserved under which name?
questions.explanation: Gandhi's residence at the ashram is known as Hriday Kunj and is preserved as a memorial.

questions.q: Gandhi is said to have vowed not to return to the Sabarmati Ashram until India achieved what?
questions.explanation: Setting out on the Salt March, Gandhi pledged not to return to the ashram until India had won its freedom.

questions.q: Roughly how long was the route of the Dandi March that began at Sabarmati Ashram?
questions.explanation: The march from the ashram to Dandi covered roughly 240 miles, about 385 kilometres.

questions.q: Life at Sabarmati Ashram was governed by a set of vows that residents were expected to follow, including which of these?
questions.explanation: Ashram residents pledged to observe vows such as truth, non-violence, and simple living.

questions.q: The ashram became an important base during which broader campaign that the Salt March launched?
questions.explanation: The Salt March from the ashram opened the Civil Disobedience Movement of 1930.

questions.q: Which social cause was strongly promoted from Sabarmati Ashram, in keeping with Gandhi's calling its residents 'Harijans'?
questions.explanation: Gandhi used the ashram to advance his campaign against untouchability, terming the depressed classes 'Harijans'.

questions.q: Gandhi located the ashram on the Sabarmati bank partly because he wished it to be situated where, in his view?
options: Between a jail and a cremation ground, fit for seekers of truth
questions.explanation: Gandhi remarked that the site between a jail and a crematorium suited a seeker of truth ready for either fate.

questions.q: Which everyday tool symbolizing self-reliance was a constant feature of daily routine at Sabarmati Ashram?
questions.explanation: The charkha was central to the ashram's daily routine, embodying Gandhi's ideal of self-reliance.

questions.q: How many companions accompanied Gandhi when he first set out from Sabarmati Ashram on the Dandi March?
questions.explanation: Gandhi began the march with a group of roughly 78 selected ashram volunteers, with thousands joining along the way.

questions.q: The Sabarmati Ashram of 1917 was relocated from Kochrab in part because of an outbreak of which disease at the earlier site?
questions.explanation: An outbreak of plague near the Kochrab site contributed to the decision to move the ashram to the Sabarmati river bank.

questions.q: Sabarmati Ashram is preserved today as a memorial commonly referred to by which institutional name?
options: Sabarmati Ashram Preservation and Memorial Trust
questions.explanation: The site is maintained by the Sabarmati Ashram Preservation and Memorial Trust as a museum and memorial.

questions.q: After Gandhi left it during the 1930s civil disobedience period, the ashram's land was for a time intended to serve what purpose?
options: Work for the welfare of Harijans
questions.explanation: Gandhi suggested the ashram land be used for Harijan welfare work after he vowed not to return until freedom was won.

questions.q: Which museum building, designed by architect Charles Correa, was added at the Sabarmati Ashram complex?
questions.explanation: The Gandhi Smarak Sangrahalaya at the ashram, housing Gandhi memorabilia, was designed by Charles Correa.

questions.q: Gandhi described the ideal of the ashram community in terms of which combination of pursuits?
questions.explanation: The ashram emphasized self-discipline, manual labour such as spinning and cleaning, and selfless service.

questions.q: Among the constructive activities pursued at the ashram was the production and promotion of which item to support village economies?
questions.explanation: The ashram promoted hand-spun khadi as a means of supporting village self-reliance and resisting imported cloth.

questions.q: The Sabarmati Ashram is considered significant in the freedom struggle chiefly because it served as which of the following?
options: A short-lived camp used only once
The nerve centre of Gandhi's movements for many years
questions.explanation: For over a decade the Sabarmati Ashram acted as the nerve centre from which Gandhi directed his major campaigns.
